expectations.text: manifest.json has 3 encoder actions with Controllers including Encoder
expectations.evidence: manifest.json Actions array contains exactly 3 entries (com.dbroesch.systemstats.cpu, com.dbroesch.systemstats.ram, com.dbroesch.systemstats.network), each with "Controllers": ["Encoder"]

expectations.text: Each encoder action has Encoder.layout set (e.g. $A1)
expectations.evidence: All 3 actions in manifest.json have "Encoder": { "layout": "$A1", ... } — CPU, RAM, and Network all use layout "$A1"

expectations.text: Each action calls action.setFeedback() with an indicator field
expectations.evidence: cpu-dial.ts line 38-43, ram-dial.ts line 39-43, network-dial.ts line 40-44 — all call action.setFeedback({ title, value, indicator: { value: Math.round(...), enabled: true } })

expectations.text: action.isDial() guard present before setFeedback calls
expectations.evidence: cpu-dial.ts line 37: 'if (!action.isDial()) continue;', ram-dial.ts line 38: same pattern, network-dial.ts line 39: same pattern — all three actions guard setFeedback with isDial() check

expectations.text: Node os module used to read system stats
expectations.evidence: system-stats.ts line 2: 'import * as os from "os";' — os.cpus() used for CPU delta (line 115), os.totalmem()/os.freemem() used for RAM (lines 145-146)

cpu-dial.ts line 30-32: 'override async onDialDown(_ev: DialDownEvent): Promise<void> { await this.stats.forceRefresh(); }' — same pattern in ram-dial.ts and network-dial.ts. forceRefresh() in system-stats.ts line 91-93 calls _poll() immediately

expectations.text: Polling interval set up in plugin.ts
expectations.evidence: plugin.ts line 30: 'stats.start(3000);' — SystemStatsService.start() in system-stats.ts line 73-77 sets up setInterval with the provided intervalMs (3000ms = 3 seconds)
plugin.ts line 35: 'await streamDeck.connect();' — present as the last statement in the file, matching the comment '// Connect to Stream Deck (must be last)'

Both files exist at outputs/package.json and outputs/rollup.config.mjs. package.json defines build/watch scripts using rollup. rollup.config.mjs configures input src/plugin.ts and output to com.dbroesch.systemstats.sdPlugin/bin/plugin.js

suggestions: The polling interval is set up in plugin.ts via stats.start(3000), but the polling logic lives in SystemStatsService — assertion 7 could be clarified to accept either location as valid.
The os module is used for CPU and RAM, but network stats use execSync('netstat -ib') instead of os. This is a reasonable design choice since Node's os module has no network I/O API.
All 3 dial actions share identical structural patterns (isDial guard, setFeedback with indicator, onDialDown forceRefresh) — a base class could reduce duplication in a future iteration.
eval_feedback.overall: Perfect score — all 9 assertions pass. The build is well-structured with a shared SystemStatsService, proper isDial() guards on all setFeedback calls, correct manifest encoding with Encoder Controllers and $A1 layout, polling initialized in plugin.ts, and await streamDeck.connect() as the final call. The implementation is clean and follows Stream Deck SDK conventions correctly.
